steps.title: Identify the key signal
steps.content: The defenders counter the charge of a “distinctively modern tendency” by pointing out that “reimaginings of the myths for new cultural contexts are almost as old as the myths themselves.”

steps.title: Apply the signal to the choices
steps.content:
bringing female perspectives to the forefront is not indicative of a novel attitude regarding fidelity to Greek and Roman myths' ideologies. states exactly what this defenders' argument supports: if myths have been reimagined for new cultural contexts since ancient times, then bringing female perspectives to these stories “is not indicative of a novel attitude”—meaning it's not new.

The other choices either claim more than the defenders established (B asserts a tradition of female-focused adaptations when only general myth adaptation was shown to be ancient), directly contradict the defenders (C frames this approach as a recent shift), or address something unstated (D focuses on motivation).

That matches the correct answer — A is correct.
